author: Dr. Lizard
title: What board exam strategy helps distinguish between ICC and WHO-5 MDS classifications?
answer: Check blast count first, then TP53 VAF and allelic status, then SF3B1, then count dysplastic lineages.
detail: Blast count determines whether the case falls into MDS, MDS/AML, or AML range initially.
TP53 VAF at least 10% with complex karyotype triggers ICC multi-hit equivalent pathway.
SF3B1 presence requires excluding del(5q), monosomy 7, and complex karyotype before assigning category.
Dysplastic lineage count determines MDS NOS-SLD versus MDS NOS-MLD if no defining genetics present.
releaseNote: This card summarizes the key points for distinguishing between ICC and WHO-5 MDS classifications on a board exam, including blast count, TP53 VAF, SF3B1 presence, and dysplastic lineage count.
